title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran conflict, now in its sixth week since February 28 airstrikes by US and Israeli forces, features intensified US air and naval operations targeting Iranian military sites, including recent strikes on Kharg Island's defenses and bridges like the B1 supply route. US special forces conducted verified ground insertions April 3-5 to rescue two airmen downed by Iranian missiles deep inside Iran, marking the first confirmed boots-on-the-ground entries amid combat aircraft losses and over 7,800 US strikes. President Trump issued an April 7 deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face attacks on power plants, with reports of a possible two-week ceasefire under discussion; Pentagon reinforcements including 82nd Airborne paratroopers and Marines position for potential escalations like island seizures, fueling trader focus on invasion thresholds.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's April 7 announcement of a conditional two-week pause in U.S. strikes on Iran—tied to Tehran's reopening of the Strait of Hormuz—marks the latest diplomatic offramp amid escalating hostilities that began with U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iranian nuclear and military sites in late February 2026. Iran has rejected prior ceasefire proposals, including a 15-point U.S. plan mediated by Pakistan, while launching missile and drone barrages at Israel and U.S. bases in the region, downing aircraft and prompting threats of intensified bombing. Pakistan's push for a phased 45-day truce offers fragile progress, but Israel's ongoing operations and Iran's defiance sustain uncertainty; trader consensus hinges on Hormuz compliance and next-week talks, with oil markets reacting sharply to de-escalation signals.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain edges trader consensus at 16% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup following the completion of global qualifiers on March 31, with all top contenders—France, England, Argentina, Brazil, and Portugal—securing spots unscathed amid playoff drama that favored underdogs like Bosnia and Herzegovina, Sweden, Czechia, and Türkiye over Italy, Poland, and Denmark. The latest FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Rankings released early April place France atop, trailed closely by Spain (2nd), Argentina (3rd), and England (4th) within a narrow points gap, underscoring the parity driving tight odds in this 48-team expanded field. Recent friendlies, including France's 2-1 upset of Brazil and Spain's 3-0 rout of Serbia, reinforced depth without shifting dominance, while group draw paths defer clashes among seeds until semifinals, heightening knockout unpredictability for defending champions Argentina and surging Euro winners Spain.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 7, 2026 between Real Madrid CF and FC Bayern München.

markets.question: Will Real Madrid CF win on 2026-04-07?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 7, 2026
If Real Madrid CF wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

If FC Bayern München wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".

This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

markets.groupItemTitle: Draw (Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München)

eventMetadata.context_description: In the UEFA Champions League quarterfinal first leg at Santiago Bernabéu, FC Bayern München's 2-1 victory over Real Madrid CF has driven trader consensus to a 100% implied probability on Bayern's win, reflecting the confirmed full-time result after 90 minutes plus stoppage time. Luis Díaz opened the scoring in the 41st minute, Harry Kane—returning from injury—netted the crucial second goal at 46' and assisted the opener, while Kylian Mbappé pulled one back for Real Madrid at 74'; Manuel Neuer's nine saves proved decisive in denying a comeback. This away result, Bayern's third straight UCL win, solidifies market pricing via the wisdom of crowds, with negligible paths to reversal barring extraordinary official protests or VAR overhauls.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 25.4% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, reflecting his national profile as California governor, term-limited after 2026, vocal opposition to President Trump, and recent SXSW remarks in mid-March hinting at a bid contingent on Democratic midterm gains. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 8.7%, drawing progressive support amid centrist pushback from groups like Third Way, which mobilized in early March to promote electable moderates over left-wing figures. Jon Ossoff's 5.7% reflects Georgia incumbency advantages in a swing state. Kamala Harris lags at 4.3% due to 2024 electoral baggage. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm outcomes, fundraising edges, and polling shifts in battleground primaries, with no incumbent creating a wide-open field.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler holds a slim edge as trader consensus favorite at 13.5% implied probability to claim a third green jacket at Augusta National, buoyed by his elite course history including 2022 and 2024 victories plus top-10 proximity from 100-125 yards, despite swing struggles and a recent Houston Open withdrawal for family reasons that limited prep time. Jon Rahm (7.8%) and Bryson DeChambeau (6.5%) have surged on scorching LIV Golf form—Rahm's Hong Kong win ending an 18-month drought and league-leading GIR rate, DeChambeau's playoff triumphs showcasing par-5 power—while Rory McIlroy (6.5%), the defending champion, returns from a back injury that sidelined him briefly. The bunched top reflects a wide-open field amid injury concerns like Collin Morikawa's lingering issues post-Valero withdrawal, uncertain favorites' rust, and no dominant strokes gained trends, heightening upset potential in this pressure-packed major.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (LOW) $110 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and the final trading day during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil spot prices have surged above $115 per barrel as of April 7, 2026, driven by severe supply disruptions from Strait of Hormuz constraints—curtailing 15-20% of global flows—and escalating US-Iran tensions, including strikes on Iran's Kharg Island export terminal. This geopolitical risk premium overrides rising US inventories, which climbed to near three-year highs in the latest EIA report for the week ending March 27, and OPEC+ production hikes of 206,000 barrels per day implemented this month. Extreme backwardation in physical markets underscores near-term tightness, fueling trader consensus for elevated April highs despite robust US output. Key catalysts ahead include tomorrow's EIA petroleum status report and potential US deadline resolutions on Hormuz access.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: The US-Israel-Iran conflict, initiated on February 28, 2026, remains active into its sixth week with no resolution in sight, as Iran rejected a US ceasefire proposal on April 6 to pause hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, instead submitting a 10-point plan via Pakistan for a permanent end to the war. Ongoing US and Israeli airstrikes targeted Iranian military sites, petrochemical facilities, and IRGC leaders, while Iran fired missiles at Israel, heightening escalation risks. President Trump extended a deadline to April 7 evening for Hormuz reopening, threatening strikes on power plants amid mediator pushes for 45-day or two-week truces. Traders eye tonight's outcome for de-escalation signals or intensified military action.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge over California Governor Gavin Newsom in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election, with implied probabilities tightly clustered around 17-18% amid a fragmented, wide-open field and no declared candidates. This positioning reflects Vance's incumbency-like advantage as the sitting vice president in the Trump administration and Newsom's elevated national profile from high-visibility policy clashes, bolstered by recent March polls showing him leading Kamala Harris in a California primary matchup and edging Vance in hypotheticals. Marco Rubio's 10% share stems from his rising visibility as Secretary of State, closing the gap in Republican nominee markets. The race remains fluid ahead of 2026 midterms, where party control of Congress and key governorships could solidify frontrunners through turnout trends, endorsements, and legislative wins; scandals, health issues, or early fundraising surges might create separation.

eventMetadata.context_description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. leads trader consensus at 49% for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination on Polymarket, outpacing Vice President J.D. Vance (38%) and Secretary of State Marco Rubio (22%), though this diverges from early polls favoring Vance. Recent U.S. airstrikes on Iran have spotlighted Rubio's foreign policy role, boosting him to 35% in the March CPAC straw poll and drawing louder cheers from Trump donors in informal Mar-a-Lago polling over Vance. RFK Jr. speculation intensified after his cousin's March claim of a "definite" run, despite his denials, amid his "Make America Healthy Again" push resonating with the base. Incumbent President Trump faces term limits, with primaries starting early 2028.

title: Sporting CP vs. Arsenal FC
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 7, 2026 between Sporting CP and Arsenal FC.

markets.question: Will Sporting CP win on 2026-04-07?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 7, 2026
If Sporting CP wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's stoppage-time 1-0 away win over Sporting CP in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg, sealed by Kai Havertz's 90'+1 poke past Rui Silva after Gabriel Martinelli's through ball, has locked trader consensus at full probability on their victory at Estádio José Alvalade. Pre-match favoritism stemmed from Arsenal's dominant 5-1 group stage rout of Sporting last season, Viktor Gyökeres' seamless integration since his 2025 summer move from the hosts, and returns of Declan Rice and Gabriel Magalhães despite Bukayo Saka and Jurrien Timber absences; Arsenal rebounded from back-to-back defeats to Manchester City and Southampton. Sporting's 17-game home unbeaten streak and perfect Champions League record there fueled slim 0.1% prices on upset or draw until the late decider. No realistic challenges persist barring rare VAR reversal or administrative anomaly.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' early-season dominance in the 2026 F1 Constructors' Championship, leading by 45 points, positions teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli as overwhelming trader favorites for the Drivers' title, capturing over 74% implied probability combined. Antonelli's back-to-back victories in China and Japan—capitalizing on safety car timing from pole in Suzuka—propelled him to the standings lead with 72 points, nine clear of Russell's 63, marking him as the youngest championship leader ever. However, Russell's experience edges trader consensus despite recent setbacks like a battery failure and P4 in Japan, with Ferrari's Charles Leclerc (49 points) trailing amid McLaren and Red Bull struggles. Toto Wolff's directive for a clean intra-team title fight underscores the tight battle ahead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder command 39.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorites, having clinched the Western Conference No. 1 seed with a league-best 62-16 record amid Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP frontrunner campaign and consistent dominance through the final stretch. San Antonio Spurs follow at 15.3% after surging to the No. 2 West seed on a 27-3 hot streak since February, fueled by Victor Wembanyama's postseason-ready explosiveness that's drawn scout praise as a title X-factor. Boston Celtics (11.4%) hold strong as East No. 2 seeds despite injury questions, while Denver Nuggets (10.5%) benefit from a late eight-game win streak; Eastern leaders like Detroit Pistons (5.9%, No. 1 East at 57-22) trail amid West-heavy sentiment as playoffs open April 18.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Celtics host the Charlotte Hornets at TD Garden with a clean injury report—the first of the season—confirming Jayson Tatum, Jaylen Brown, Derrick White, and the full rotation available, bolstering their 53-25 record and No. 2 Eastern Conference standing amid late-season seeding push. Hornets (43-36, eighth in East) enter on a four-game win streak, including a 122-108 road upset at Minnesota behind LaMelo Ball's 35 points, but face questions on Coby White (groin, probable) and PJ Hall (out, ankle) while chasing play-in security. Recent head-to-head split favors neither decisively, though Boston's home-court edge, superior defense, and health tilt trader consensus toward the hosts in this playoff implications matchup.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chicago Bulls head to Capital One Arena as modest trader favorites against the injury-riddled Washington Wizards, both eliminated from playoffs with Bulls at 29-49 on a seven-game skid and Wizards at 17-61 mired in late-season struggles. Washington's official injury report lists around 10 players out or questionable, including key pieces like Alex Sarr and Tristan Vukcevic (knee), thrusting rookies Julian Reese and Will Riley into prominent roles after recent bright spots like Riley's 30-point outing. Chicago counters absences of Anfernee Simons (wrist), Josh Giddey (hamstring), and Matas Buzelis (illness), but expects Collin Sexton and Patrick Williams (probable, thumb) in the lineup alongside projected starters Tre Jones and Isaac Okoro. Momentum favors no one decisively in this low-stakes tilt, with Wizards' home advantage and Bulls' travel as pivotal swings.

eventMetadata.context_description: Real Madrid and Bayern Munich head into their UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg on April 7 at the Bernabéu amid injury crises tilting trader sentiment toward a competitive tie. Madrid's Thibaut Courtois is ruled out for both legs with a thigh injury, forcing Andriy Lunin into goal, while Rodrygo remains sidelined long-term by an ACL tear and Éder Militão nears return from a muscle issue around April 1. Bayern faces a goalkeeper nightmare with Manuel Neuer (calf), Sven Ulreich (adductor), and Jonas Urbig (concussion) unavailable, plus Jamal Musiala out with a fibula injury. Madrid's home advantage and recent La Liga momentum counter Bayern's strong aggregate win over Atalanta, but vulnerabilities on both benches heighten upset potential.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Minnesota Timberwolves, clinging to the sixth seed in the Western Conference at 46-32 amid a three-game losing streak, face a severely depleted Indiana Pacers squad (18-60, 14th in East) on their home floor. Critical injury blows hit Minnesota hardest with star Anthony Edwards (knee) and defender Jaden McDaniels (knee) ruled out, potentially hampering their top-10 paint scoring led by Julius Randle. Indiana lists nine players on the injury report, including out statuses for Pascal Siakam (ankle), Andrew Nembhard (back), T.J. McConnell (hamstring), Aaron Nesmith (neck), and Ben Sheppard (hip), exacerbating their league-worst defense allowing 120.7 points per game. Timberwolves hold a road edge (21-17 away) and won their only prior matchup 114-110, but both teams' roster attrition underscores upset potential in this late-season clash with playoff implications for Minnesota.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Preliminary official escrutinio results from Colombia's March 8, 2026, legislative elections, certified by the Registraduría Nacional across most departments, position the Historic Pact for Colombia (PH) first and Centro Democrático (CD) second in Chamber of Representatives seats, with Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) securing third place at approximately 25 seats—well ahead of Partido Conservador Colombiano's 20. Trader consensus prices PLC at 96% implied probability for third-most seats under proportional representation by department, driven by consistent departmental tallies and minimal post-election disputes. Final certification, expected soon after late March declarations for nearly all regions (pending Chocó), reinforces this; realistic challenges include rare recounts reallocating curules or successful legal appeals overturning departmental outcomes, though margins and historical patterns suggest low risk of upheaval.

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $60,000 in April?

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US airstrikes overnight targeted over 50 military sites on Kharg Island, Iran's primary oil export terminal handling 90% of its crude shipments, but explicitly spared energy infrastructure according to US officials. Iranian state media released footage confirming the island remains fully under armed forces control with uninterrupted exports, countering escalation claims. President Trump's fresh threats of broader retaliation, tied to an impending deadline on nuclear talks and regional proxies, fuel speculation of potential seizure or intensified operations. Traders assess low near-term loss-of-control risk amid Iran's defensive reinforcements and global oil price volatility, eyeing upcoming diplomatic signals or further military actions as key catalysts.

title: Kansas City Royals vs. Cleveland Guardians
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Kansas City Royals and Cleveland Guardians, scheduled for April 7 at 6:10PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Kansas City Royals" if the Kansas City Royals win the game.

This market will resolve to "Cleveland Guardians" if the Cleveland Guardians win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kansas City Royals enter game two of their AL Central series at Progressive Field with a 1-0 lead after Monday's 4-2 win behind Michael Wacha's six scoreless innings and solo homers from Jonathan India and Kyle Jensen, boosting their record to 5-5 while Guardians sit at 6-5. Probable starters Noah Cameron (3.05 ERA in 16 career AL starts) for Kansas City face Gavin Williams (3.18 ERA across three prior outings vs. Royals) for Cleveland, whose shortstop Gabriel Arias landed on the 10-day injured list Tuesday with a left hamstring strain, prompting a top-prospect recall. Frigid Cleveland weather advanced the 1:10 p.m. ET first pitch, testing early-season lineups in a tight divisional matchup.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 8?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 97.3% implied probability to no change in the federal funds target range at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting the Fed's March pause at 3.50%-3.75% amid steady February CPI at 2.4% year-over-year and resilient labor data. March nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs—surpassing expectations—while unemployment dipped to 4.3%, bolstering the central bank's wait-and-see stance as Chair Powell noted in late March remarks on monitoring war-related energy pressures. The March dot plot still projects one cut later in 2026, but recent strength has traders dismissing near-term moves. Scenarios challenging this include hotter-than-expected April CPI or sudden labor softening, though proximity to resolution limits volatility.

title: Lightning vs. Senators
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 7 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Lightning win, the market will resolve to "Lightning".
If the Senators win, the market will resolve to "Senators".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

In the NHL game, scheduled for April 7 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Senators" if the Senators win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Lightning".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tampa Bay Lightning visit Ottawa Senators tonight at Canadian Tire Centre in a pivotal late-season Atlantic Division clash, with both squads battling defensive injuries that could dictate puck control and special teams. Lightning hold a strong 48-23-4 record and recent point streak, including a 4-2 home win over Ottawa on March 28, but face absences like Victor Hedman (LTIR), day-to-day Brandon Hagel and Anthony Cirelli, plus Pontus Holmberg's fresh upper-body injury; Andrei Vasilevskiy eyes the start. Senators (39-27-10), pushing for playoffs, confirm Linus Ullmark in net amid missing Thomas Chabot (forearm, practiced non-contact today), Tyler Kleven, and Carter Yakemchuk; Jake Sanderson anchors the blue line. Expect a gritty matchup emphasizing goaltending and depth scoring.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Utah Jazz enter their matchup against the New Orleans Pelicans severely depleted by injuries, with Lauri Markkanen (hip), Walker Kessler (shoulder), Keyonte George (hamstring), Jusuf Nurkic (nose), and Jaren Jackson Jr. (knee) ruled out per the latest NBA injury report, alongside Ace Bailey (questionable, right knee) and Kyle Filipowski (questionable, back), capping a dismal 21-58 campaign marked by recent blowouts like 146-111 to Oklahoma City and 140-106 to Houston. Pelicans (25-54) counter without Trey Murphy III (right ankle sprain) and Bryce McGowens (toe fracture), Dejounte Murray (left hand contusion, likely out), and Karlo Matkovic (low back), following narrow losses to Orlando (112-108) and Sacramento (117-113). New Orleans enjoys home-court edge and a February sweep (129-118, 115-105) in this lottery-bound finale.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 96.7% for the Iranian regime falling by April 30, reflecting its demonstrated resilience despite severe economic collapse and waves of nationwide protests that erupted in December 2025 and peaked in early 2026. The regime's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) orchestrated a brutal crackdown, including mass arrests, executions of political prisoners as recently as April 2, and suppression of dissent, restoring order without triggering mass defections or uprisings—corroborated by U.S. intelligence assessments in March deeming no imminent collapse risk. Recent escalations, such as Iran's April 6 rejection of a U.S. ceasefire proposal amid Israeli airstrikes on regime-linked facilities, have rallied pro-regime demonstrations rather than weakening control. Realistic shifts could arise from IRGC mutiny, a Supreme Leader health crisis, or overwhelming external military intervention fracturing internal cohesion before the deadline.

eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers face the Utah Mammoth in a pivotal late-season matchup at Delta Center, with both teams locked in a tight Pacific Division playoff race—Oilers at 39-29-9 (87 points) atop the standings, Mammoth at 40-30-6 (86 points) nipping closely behind despite fewer regulation wins. Oilers enter shorthanded after injuries sidelined stars Leon Draisaitl (lower body) and Zach Hyman (undisclosed, potentially out two weeks), compounded by a 5-1 home loss to Vegas that snapped their five-game win streak. Utah rides momentum from a 7-4 road win over Vancouver, bolstering their push for a wild-card spot, while Tristan Jarry starts in net for Edmonton on their final road trip. Head-to-head history favors the Oilers, but home-ice advantage and roster health tilt trader consensus toward a competitive battle.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder (62-16, Western Conference leaders) hold a decided edge over the fourth-seeded Los Angeles Lakers (50-28) entering tonight's matchup at Crypto.com Arena, driven by superior regular-season form and fewer debilitating injuries. Lakers face a depleted roster with Luka Doncic (hamstring), Austin Reaves (oblique), and Marcus Smart (ankle) ruled out, while LeBron James remains questionable (foot), straining their backcourt depth and load management late in the season. Thunder miss Jalen Williams (hamstring management) but feature a healthy core of Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Chet Holmgren, and Lu Dort, fresh off wins in prior head-to-heads. Playoff seeding implications and home-court advantage heighten stakes for both sides.

eventMetadata.context_description: Baltimore Orioles lefty Trevor Rogers (2-0, 1.38 ERA) takes the mound against Chicago White Sox righty Shane Smith in the series opener at Guaranteed Rate Field, where chilly 36°F temperatures and winds may limit offense for both struggling clubs hovering around .400 win percentages early in 2026. Orioles halted a three-game skid with a 2-1 victory over the White Sox yesterday via spot starter Brandon Young, leveraging their lopsided 12-1 head-to-head edge over the past three seasons including this year. Baltimore contends without infielder Jackson Holliday (10-day IL) and starter Zach Eflin (60-day elbow IL), while White Sox manage outfield injuries; low runs per game (under four) underscore pitching-dominant matchup dynamics shaping trader consensus.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's "Liekinheitin" by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen has surged to a commanding 38.6% implied probability on Polymarket as the clear frontrunner for Eurovision 2026, propelled by their unanimous jury and public victory at UMK on February 28 amid protests, blending pop-rock energy with powerhouse vocals that appeal to both juries and televoters. The duo's music video and staging previews have fueled fan polls like OGAE and The Model projections, where Finland leads by 36 points, cementing trader consensus amid national final wrap-ups. Denmark (10.3%) and France (9.3%) trail with strong entries gaining jury traction, while Australia and Greece hover around 6-7% on Delta Goodrem's "Eclipse" and Akylas's "Ferto." Momentum could shift with semi-final running orders ahead of Vienna's May 12-16 showdown.
